intentional_test_features: Classic paroxysmal SVT/pre-excitation history: abrupt-onset and abrupt-offset ('like a switch flipped'), perfectly regular, very fast palpitations with presyncope in a young structurally-normal heart - the episode has already self-terminated and the patient is now in sinus rhythm and stable, so this tests recognition from history rather than acute rhythm management

Recurrent self-terminating episodes (3 this year) in a young healthy adult - the AI Doctor should recognise a re-entrant paroxysmal SVT pattern and arrange a priority resting 12-lead ECG to look for a pre-excitation pattern, not dismiss it as anxiety/panic
Trigger identification: energy drinks / pre-workout stimulants / heavy caffeine as a modifiable precipitant to address

Safety-netting for a future episode: coach a modified Valsalva as a self-help manoeuvre, and escalate to 000 if a future episode does not self-terminate, causes actual syncope, or is accompanied by severe chest pain

Pharmacology-check feature: the diagnostic finding (if pre-excitation is present) changes the emergency drug safety of any future episode - relevant if the patient ever presents in a fast irregular rhythm - so the AI Doctor should NOT recommend AV-nodal blocking self-medication and should route to a priority ECG and cardiology/electrophysiology assessment

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
